facts.statement: Merck & Co., Inc. trades on the NYSE under ticker MRK and is classified in the SEC submissions metadata under the SIC description "Pharmaceutical Preparations."
Merck describes itself as a research-intensive biopharmaceutical company focused on delivering innovative health solutions that advance the prevention and treatment of diseases in people and animals.
Merck reported FY2025 revenue of $60.115 billion, filed February 24, 2026 under the Revenues concept.
Merck reported FY2025 net income of $365 million, filed February 24, 2026 under the NetIncomeLoss concept.
Merck reported FY2025 net cash provided by operating activities of $13.006 billion.
Merck reported FY2025 research and development expense of $30.531 billion.
At FY2025, Merck reported stockholders' equity of $46.313 billion, long-term debt of $34.462 billion, and cash and equivalents of $13.242 billion.

Merck stated in its 2026 proxy that it had approximately 80 ongoing Phase 3 studies across multiple therapeutic areas and modalities and saw an opportunity to launch over 20 potential new growth drivers.

Merck stated that the FDA approved KEYTRUDA QLEX, enabling subcutaneous administration of KEYTRUDA across all solid tumor indications, and that the European Commission approved subcutaneous administration for KEYTRUDA across all adult indications in Europe.

Merck is developing sacituzumab tirumotecan, or sac-TMT, an investigational anti-TROP2 antibody-drug conjugate in collaboration with Kelun-Biotech, and stated it was evaluating sac-TMT in 17 Phase 3 trials.

Merck identified enlicitide as an investigational oral PCSK9 inhibitor for adults with high LDL cholesterol, WINREVAIR as a medicine for adults with pulmonary arterial hypertension, and OHTUVAYRE as a first-in-class maintenance treatment for adults with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease obtained through the Verona Pharma acquisition.

Merck stated it launched ENFLONSIA in the U.S. as an RSV preventive option for infants during their first RSV season without weight-based dosing, continued to see increased uptake of adult pneumococcal vaccine CAPVAXIVE, and described GARDASIL and GARDASIL 9 as important products for preventing certain HPV-related cancers in males and females.

Merck reported positive data readouts from its HIV pipeline, including doravirine/islatravir for HIV-1 treatment, and said it initiated Phase 3 trials for alimatravir, also known as MK-8527, as a once-monthly oral HIV-1 PrEP candidate.

Merck stated it broadened tulisokibart clinical development with Phase 2b trials in three inflammatory disease indications beyond ongoing Phase 3 trials in ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, and said it is advancing ophthalmology candidates MK-3000 and MK-8748 for certain retinal diseases.

Merck described Animal Health as an important contributor to growth and stated that in 2025 it received European Commission approval of NUMELVI tablets for dogs, a second-generation JAK inhibitor for atopic dermatitis, and recently received FDA approval for NUMELVI.

Merck stated that business development is a core part of its growth strategy, that it closed the Verona Pharma acquisition in 2025, completed the acquisition of Cidara Therapeutics in January 2026, and had invested over $60 billion in business development since 2021.

Robert M. Davis signed the 2025 Form 10-K as Chairman, Chief Executive Officer and President; Caroline Litchfield signed as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer; and Dalton Smart signed as Senior Vice President Finance-Global Controller and Principal Accounting Officer.

Management, with participation from the CEO and CFO, concluded that Merck's disclosure controls and procedures were effective as of the end of FY2025, and management concluded internal control over financial reporting was effective as of December 31, 2025.

Merck's Code of Conduct applies to all employees, including the principal executive officer, principal financial officer, and principal accounting officer, and the 10-K states that every Company employee is responsible for business practices in accordance with law and ethical principles.

Merck stated that all employees are required annually to complete Code of Conduct training, and that a staff of internal auditors regularly monitors the adequacy and application of internal controls worldwide.

For incentive plans, Merck disclosed Company Scorecard weightings of 20% pipeline, 10% sustainability, 35% revenue, and 35% pre-tax income, while long-term metrics include EPS weighted 50% and relative TSR weighted 50%.
Merck stated that annual employee compensation, including senior management compensation, is derived in part using a non-GAAP pretax income metric.

As of December 31, 2025, Merck disclosed 12,476,856 securities to be issued upon exercise of outstanding options, warrants and rights under shareholder-approved equity compensation plans, with a weighted-average exercise price of $87.95 and 65,539,454 securities remaining available for future issuance.

PricewaterhouseCoopers identified U.S. rebate accruals for Medicaid, Managed Care and Medicare Part D as a critical audit matter; Merck's accrued balance for aggregate customer discounts in accrued and other current liabilities was $1.5 billion as of December 31, 2025, with the majority related to those U.S. rebate accruals.
